Tests the behavior of `Array.prototype.splice` and `Object.freeze` with string and regex operations.

# Purpose
This code snippet performs operations on JavaScript's built-in objects and includes a test assertion. It modifies the `Array.prototype` by calling `splice` with `Function.prototype` as the first argument, which is an unusual operation and may not have a meaningful effect. The `Array.prototype` is then frozen using `Object.freeze`, preventing further modifications to its properties. A new `String` object is created with the value "Hello", and the `split` method is called on it using a new `RegExp` object, resulting in an array. The code concludes with an assertion that checks if the length of the resulting array is 5, which verifies the expected behavior of the `split` method when applied to the string "Hello".
